english: empty, by your quitting it Mil. śul-du lús-pai nor (shul du lus pa'i nor) all the things left behind in the camp Glr.; dóṅ-tse láṅs-pai śul-du (dong tse langs pa'i shul du) instead of the coin which had been taken away (there appeared . . .) Dzl.; kyód-kyi śul-du (khyod kyi shul du) in the place which you occupied during your life Thgr.; hence in a looser sense: btsún-mo méd-pai śul-du (btsun mo med pa'i shul du) on the occasion of the queen's absence Glr.; in the same manner Tar. 103, 16, 19, and also thus: dei śul-du (de'i shul du) Glr. 51 during her absence. — 2. track, rut, of a carriage, furrow, of a plough Dzl., way, road; also in a gen. sense: śul ťag-ríṅ (shul thag ring) a long way Glr.; śul-lám (shul lam) = śul (shul); acc. to Cs. also manner, method. — 3. any thing left behind by a person departed, or by a thing removed, as ču-śul (chu shul), mar-śul (mar shul), pye-śul (phye shul) that little water, butter or flour which adheres to the vessel emptied, but not washed; me-śul (me shul) the extinguished cinders left by a fire; property left by a deceased person śul tṣáṅ-ma yóg-po-la ťob (shul gtsang ma g.yog po la thob) his servant gets all the property left (by his master) W., C., pa-śul (pha shul) paternal inheritance, patrimony; pa-śul-ᵒdzin-pa (pha shul 'dzin pa) the heir C.; śul yaṅ mi ᵒdug (shul yang mi 'dug) nothing at all is left; *śul-med-kaṅ čo* (shul med kang co) W. finish it at once! eat it all up! śu-tsaṅ-po (shu gtsang po) one that eats all up, clears his trencher (a good trencher-man) Ts.

Cs.: backbone, back, posteriors; śul-šá (shul sha) the flesh, the muscles of the back, śul-rgyús (shul rgyus) the fibres, the nerves of the back; Sch.: bśul-drí (bshul dri) smell of excrements, śul-byí (shul byi) polecat, fitchet.
1. v. śú-ba (shu ba). — 2. śus ᵒdébs-pa (shus 'debs pa) to whistle S.g.
any thing copied, a copy Cs.
1. Cs. śe-stag (she stag), śe-dag (she dag) = ša-stág (sha stag) mere, only, nothing but. — 2. num.: 117.
Cs.: = ťo-yig (tho yig) a kind of contract or bargain.
(for rjé-ma (rje ma) or rjés-ma? (rjes ma?)) W. noblewoman, lady of rank or quality, lady, *śé-ma čuṅ-ṅu* (she ma chung ngu), *śem-čuṅ* (shem chung) nobleman's daughter, young lady, Miss.
Sch.: 'divine predestination, divine protection; nature, fate, destiny; power; origin of power or authority; strength', force, the latter signification also in Wts. (?).
1. imp. of gśégs-pa (gshegs pa), resp. for šog (shog). — 2. the Arabian شيخ, chieftain, elder, senior. — 3. C. col. for śed (shed) I.

I. strength, force, = stóbs (stobs), mťu (mthu), C. also śeg (sheg); dpá-žiṅ śed-čé (dpa' zhing shed che) a mighty hero Thgy.; śéd-čan (shed can) strong, vigorous, powerful; śéd-mo (shed mo) 1. sbst. = śed (shed)? 2. adj. = śéd-čan (shed can) Ts., *śé-mo gyág-pa yin* (she mo rgyag pa yin) he is strong and stout, śed-méd (shed med) powerless, weak, śed-méd-kyi rtá-bas rkaṅ-ťáṅ mgyogs (shed med kyi rta bas rkang thang mgyogs) one travels quicker on foot than on a weak horse; śed-čúṅ (shed chung) weak, feeble, frail, e.g. lus (lus) Lt.; śed ᵒbri (shed 'bri) strength decreases, begins to fail, gsos (gsos) is restored, nyams (nyams) is impaired; śed skyéd-pa (shed skyed pa) to grow fat Sch.: 'to protect; to make haste'; *mi žig-la śed čúg-če* (mi zhig la shed cug ce) W. to strengthen a person; *śed daṅ nyé-če* (shed dang nye ce) W. to rub well, forcibly; *śed žár-te (sbyar-te) čoṅ* (shed zhar te (sbyar te) cong) run and jump! *śed žár-te gyob* (shed zhar te gyob) swing your arm and throw! W.; *śed-kyer-nág-po* (shed khyer nag po) by force, with violence, e.g. *taṅ* (tang) he forced it on (me) W. (cf. nan (nan)); śed-po-čé (shed po che) a strong, powerful man Thgy.; śéd-bu (shed bu) Lex. id.; śed-bdág (shed bdag) Sch. one having power or authority, a lord, ruler. — *śed-wáṅ* (shed wang) W. force, violence, *śed-wáṅ daṅ* (shed wang dang) by force, e.g. to take, *śed-wáṅ táṅ-če* (shed wang tang ce) W. to violate, to force (a girl). II. the approximate direction, region, quarter, nyí-mai ᵒog śéd-na (nyi ma'i 'og shed na) below the sun, i.e. between the sun and the horizon Mil.; W.: *gaṅ śéd-la* (gang shed la) in what direction? where-to? *de śéd-la* (de shed la) about in that direction; *gaṅ śed nẹ (lit. gnas) žig-tu* (gang shed ne (gnas) zhig tu) to some place or other.
(?) floor of a house or room W.
abbreviation for śes-ráb-kyi pá-rol-tu pyín-pa (shes rab kyi pha rol tu phyin pa), the title of a division of the Kan-gyur (bka' 'gyur).
